div.p: Protecting nodes in lock-free structures requires publishing the hazard pointer BEFORE dereferencing

g.text: T2: Thread 2 Tries to Free Node A

div.p: The hazard pointer doesn't protect a specific node—it protects whatever is at that address right now. If the address changes between setting and loading, validation fails and we retry. This ensures the node we dereference is the same one we protected.
stack->top → Node A (valid) Thread 1 hazard_slot[0] = NULL Thread 2 hazard_slot[0] = NULL
Thread 1: hazard_protect(0, top) // slot[0] = &Node A Thread 1 hazard_slot[0] = Node A
Thread 2: pop() succeeds, retires Node A Thread 2: hazard_scan_and_reclaim() → Sees Thread 1's slot[0] = Node A → CANNOT free Node A (protected!)
Thread 1: validates node == slot (yes) Thread 1: reads node->value // Safe! Thread 1: hazard_release(0)
Between load and protect, another thread can:

text.tspan: // Publish what we're about to load
// Publish what we're about to load
